questions.question: At the position of attention, where should the thumbs be placed?
options: Along the trouser seams with thumbs pointing downward
Along the trouser seams with thumbs wrapped around the fingers
Curled along the trouser seams with fingertips touching the leg
Behind the back with thumbs interlocked

questions.question: When given the command 'Parade, REST,' where are the hands placed?
options: At the sides along the trouser seams
Crossed in front of the chest
On the hips with fingers pointing downward
Behind the back, right hand inside the left, just below the waist

questions.question: What is the difference between 'At Ease' and 'Rest'?
options: At Ease requires silence; Rest permits talking
At Ease allows talking; Rest does not
Both allow free movement and talking
There is no difference between the two commands

questions.question: Which foot remains stationary during the commands At Ease and Rest?
options: Both feet must remain in place

questions.question: How many counts are involved in executing 'Right Face'?
During 'About Face,' on which foot's heel and which foot's toe does the pivot occur?
What is the cadence for quick time marching?
What is the length of a step in quick time?
What is the cadence for double time marching?
During half step, what is the approximate step length?
On which foot is the command of execution 'MARCH' given when initiating mark time from quick time?
options: It does not matter which foot

questions.question: What are the two parts of a drill command?
options: Preparatory command and command of execution

questions.question: What is a 'supplementary command'?
options: A command used only during ceremonies
A written directive issued before drill practice
A command given by the senior officer present
An oral command given by a subordinate leader that reinforces or relays the commander's command

questions.question: In a flight formation, what is the 'element'?
options: The smallest drill unit comprised of at least 3 but normally 4 members in a column
A single file of personnel from front to rear
The space between two adjacent members

questions.question: What is the normal interval between members when in formation?
What is 'close interval'?
options: Shoulder to shoulder with no space
Four inches between the shoulder and elbow of adjacent members

questions.question: What is the approximate distance between ranks in a formation?
When rendering the hand salute, the upper arm should be at what angle relative to the ground?
When saluting, the tip of the right forefinger should touch where?
options: The left eyebrow or the front left corner of the headgear
The right eyebrow or the front right corner of the headgear

questions.question: Who initiates the salute between two individuals of different rank?
options: Either individual may initiate the salute

questions.question: What command is used to dismiss a flight from formation?
During a column movement, what command changes the direction of march 90 degrees to the right?
What is the difference between a column movement and a flanking movement?
options: Column movements change direction 45 degrees; flanking movements change 90 degrees
Column movements are used only in ceremonies; flanking movements are for drill
There is no difference; the terms are interchangeable
In a column movement the lead element pivots and others follow; in a flanking movement all members pivot simultaneously

questions.question: On which foot does the command of execution 'MARCH' fall when executing 'Right Flank, MARCH'?
What command is given to align a flight in ranks with arm's-length interval?
After 'Dress Right, DRESS,' what command returns personnel to the position of attention?
What is the command to open ranks for inspection?
During the retreat ceremony, what action is taken at the first note of 'To the Color' or the national anthem?
options: All personnel outdoors face the flag (or music) and salute
The flight commander gives the command 'Parade, REST'
All personnel stand at ease until the music ends

questions.question: What bugle call signals the end of the official duty day and accompanies the lowering of the flag?
What bugle call is played at the beginning of the official duty day and accompanies the raising of the flag?
During a change of command ceremony, what object is passed to symbolize the transfer of authority?
How many rifle volleys are traditionally fired during a military funeral honors ceremony?
What bugle call is played at military funerals after the rifle volleys?
Who carries the guidon in a flight formation?
options: The element leader of the first element
The guidon bearer, positioned to the left of and one step behind the flight commander

questions.question: When the flight is given 'Present, ARMS,' how does the guidon bearer render the salute?
options: By executing a standard hand salute with the free hand
By lowering the guidon to a 45-degree angle from the vertical
By passing the guidon to the nearest member and saluting

questions.question: What is the proper arm swing while marching at quick time?
options: Arms swing 9 inches to the front and 6 inches to the rear of the trouser seam
Arms swing 6 inches to the front and 3 inches to the rear of the trouser seam
Arms swing 3 inches to the front and 3 inches to the rear
Arms remain at the sides without swinging

questions.question: What is the command to halt a flight that is marching at quick time?
When marching, on which foot does the 'HALT' command of execution fall?
What command is given to assemble personnel into formation?
What does the command 'COVER' direct members in a formation to do?
options: Align themselves directly behind the person in front of them at the proper distance
Close the interval between ranks to shoulder width
